key_insight: Off-the-shelf multimodal LLMs can zero-shot identify stress-critical regions in 3D CAD geometry from physics-guided prompts, making them a promising semantic proxy for mesh refinement.
engineering_relevance: This could reduce reliance on iterative PDE-driven preprocessing or large supervised surrogates for adaptive finite element meshing, helping prioritize compute where it matters most.
method: Uses multimodal LLMs to localize stress-relevant regions from textual physics instructions.
Introduces orthoViews to choose 2D views that expose key 3D geometric features better to the model.
Compares against a tuned geometric heuristic under the same refinement budget.
evidence_signal: Evaluated across diverse CAD geometries and loading cases with multiple state-of-the-art MLLMs, showing higher precision than blind heuristics in selecting stress-relevant features.
watch_item: Performance depends on view selection and model grounding quality, so 2D projection limits and physics misinterpretation may still cause missed or spurious refinements.
paper_title: GReFEM: Multimodal LLMs as Zero-Shot Semantic Assistants for Physics-Guided 3D Mesh Refinement
